content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street• Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Sue Novascl 
El Dorado County Supervisor - District V 
3080 Elf Lane 
South Lake Tahoe, CA 96150 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-15-063 
Dear Ms. Novasel: 
June 29, 2015 
This letter responds to your request for ad\'ice regarding the conflict of interest provisions 
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 
Please note that we arc only providing ad\ ice under the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict 
of interest or Section I 090. This letter is based only on the facts presented. The Fair Political 
Practices Commission (the "Commission"} docs not act as a finder of fact when it renders 
advice. (/11 re Oglesby ( 1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) 
QUESTIONS 
I. Do the Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit you from making, participating in 
making and influencing decisions regarding the Meyers Arca Plan (''MAP") when you have real 
property. a business entity. and other financial interests located within the area designated by the 
plan? 
2. l f you have a conflict. what limits arc placed on your ability as a member of the El 
Dorado County Board of Supen·isors ( .. Board") to participate in the MAP? 
CONCLUSIONS 
I. Based on the fact that your real property interest is located directly within the proposed 
MAP, we find that decisions regarding the plan forcseeably affect your interests in a material 
way. Accordingly, you may not make. participate in making, or use your position to influence 
any decisions regarding the MAP. as discussed below. 
1 The Pohtu:al Rcfi.1rm Act is con tamed m GO\ c:mmcnl Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
refort!nces are to the GO\ emment Code. unless othern 1se md1catt!d. The regulations of the Fair Poliucal Practices 
Commission arc coniamed Ill Sccuons Hll 10 through 18997 ofTnlc 2 of the Cahfomia Code ofRegulauons. All 
regulatory references are to Tnle 2. D1\ 1s1on 6 of the Cal1fom1a Code of Regulations. unless othen, 1se md1cated. 


File No. A-15-063 
Page No. 2 
2. Due to your conflict, you are prohibited from voting, participating in or influencing 
decisions regarding the MAP. Howe\'er, you may speak with the press and public outside of the 
Board meetings. Additionally, the Act would not prohibit you from obtaining information 
otherwise available to the public. Howc\'er, other contacts, including those with staff, regarding a 
decision in which you have a conflict of interest may be considered "influencing" the decision. 
FACTS 
You are the County Super\'isor for El Dorado County, District V. A new area plan is 
being developed for the unincorporated community of Meyers, located within your jurisdiction. 
You described the following interests: 
• You and your husband own 23'l-o of a parcel located within the commercial strip 
area of the plan, valued at approximately $700,000. The value of your pro rata 
share of the fair market value is $161,000. 
• 
You and your husband also own 50% of a corporation, a mortgage and in\'estment 
company, located on the parcel. 
• Additionally, you and your immediate family own 1.4% of a fund that owns a 
separate piece of property, valued at $500,000, also located within the Meyers 
Arca Plan. 
• 
Lastly, your business has a secured loan on another piece of residential property 
located within the plan. 
The original Meyers Community Plan adopted in 1993 was a 155 acre commercial 
development for a 2-mile corridor of the commercial strip area. The new plan has expanded to 
approximately 669 acres which includes mixed-use, recreation. and residential areas surrounding 
and including the Meyers commercial strip. 
The MAP is a specific plan2 with elements supplementing the Lake Tahoe Regional Plan 
and the El Dorado County General Plan. It includes changes to zoning and allowable uses within 
the commercial strip. Your facts indicate there are less than I Od businesses within the 
commercial strip and approximately 3,000 residents within the MAP. According to the United 
States Census Bureau. El Dorado County has approximately 4,100 businesses and a population 
of 183.000. 
: According to the most recent draft of the Tlcycrs Area Plan. the legal authority and requm:ments for the 
MAP are pro\·ided m ''Chapter 13 (Area Plans) of the TRPA [Tahoe Regional Planning Agency] Code of 
Ordinances. and Chapter 17.22.XI (Specific Plan) of the El Dorado County Zoning Ordinance:· 


ANALYSIS 
File No. A-15-063 
Page No. 3 
Section 87100 prohibits any state or local public official from making, participating in 
making, or using his or her official position to influence a government decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. Financial interests include: 
• Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest of at least 
S2,000. (Section 87103(b).) 
• Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
at least S2,000 and any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, 
partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(a) and 
(d).) 
• Any source of income, such as a business entity, from which the official has recci ved 
income of $500 or more within 12 months before the decision. This also includes income 
from any client of the business entity of at least $500, provided to and received by the 
public official within 12 months before the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
• Any donor of gift(s) amounting to a total of at least $460 within 12 months before a 
decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
• A public official's own personal finances, or those of a member of his or her immediate 
family. (Section 87103).) 
We focus on your interests in real property. From the facts you provided. you have a 
financial interest in the parcel because you ha,·c a 23% interest worth approximately S 161.000. 
I. Foreseeability a11d Materiali(l': 
A public official has a financial interest in a government decision. within the meaning of 
the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on 
the official. the official's immediate family, or the official's interests. and it is distinguishable 
from the effect on the public generally. 
The financial effect on an official's real property interest is presumed to be reasonably 
foreseeable if the property is the subject ofa governmental decision. (Regulation 1870l(a).) Real 
property is the subject of a decision when "the decision involves the issuance. renewal. apprornl, 
denial or revocation of any license. permi't. or other entitlement to. or contract with. the financial 
interest, and includes any governmental decision affecting a real property financial interest as 
described in Regulation 18702.2(a)(l)-(6). 
A financial effect is material pursuant to Regulation l 8702.2(a) if the decision: 


File No. A-15-063 
Page No. 4 
"( 1) lnYolves the adoption of or amendment to a general ... or specific 
plan. and the parcel is located within the proposed boundaries of the plan."3 
According to the materials you submitted, the MAP will be adopted by the County Board 
of Supervisors as an amendment to the Meyers Community Plan. Your parcel is located within 
the commercial strip area of the MAP. Thus, a material financial effect is presumed to be 
reasonably foreseeable. 
Because we conclude you have a conflict of interest based on your property, we do not 
analyze your other interests. If you should dispose of your real property interest you should seek 
additional advice. 
' Public General {v 
A public official is prohibited from making. participating in making. or influencing 
governmental decisions if the reasonably foreseeable material financial effect is distinguishable 
from the public generally. 
Regulation 18703 states the financial effect is indistinguishable if"a significant segment 
of the public is affected and the effect on his or her financial interest is not unique compared to 
the effect on the significant scbrment." 
A significant segment of the public consists of at least 25° o of all real property, 
commercial real property, or residential real property within the official's jurisdiction. 
Regulation 18703(d) defines •'jurisdiction" as ''the region, county. city. district or other 
geographical area in which [the agency] has jurisdiction" or ''the designated geographical area 
the official was elected to represent, or the area to which the official's authority and duties arc 
limited if not elected." You have not provided specific facts about property in the MAP versus 
that in your election district or the county. Ho\\ ever, it would not appear that the number of 
properties in the MAP would meet the 25° o threshold in either case. Thus, the exception would 
not apply. 
3. Disq11af(licatio11 
Since you have a disqualifying financial interest, you arc prohibited from voting on. 
directly participating in, or influencing any discussions with other members of the Board of 
Supervisors. in matters concerning the MAP. 
Please note that you are similarly prohibited from rnting on. directly participating in. or 
influencing any decisions with other members of the Tahoe Regional Planning Agency·s 
'There is a narrow excepllon to this standard m Regulation IR702.2(c)(2) applicable 10 general plans. 
Your fact` indicate the a!AP ib a specific plan\\ 1th elements amending and supplementing the Lake Tahoe Regional 
Plan and the El Dorado County General Plan. Therefore. you do not meet the requirement of the exception. 


File No. A-15-063 
Page No. 5 
Governing Board. Any governmental decisions made by the Tahoe Board regarding the MAP 
will also have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your real property interest. 
Therefore, you have a disqualifying interest and must follow the same disqualification 
requirements described in Regulation 18707. 
An official is making a decision if the official "authorizes or directs any action, votes, 
appoints a person, obligates or commits his or her agency to any course of action, or enters into 
any contractual a&,rreement on behalf of his agency." (Regulation l 8704(a).) Participating in a 
decision occurs when the official "provides information. an opinion, or a recommendation for the 
purpose of affecting the decision without significant intervening substantive review." 
(Regulation l 8704(h).) Lastly, an official uses his or her position to influence a decision if the 
official contacts or appears before any official in his or her agency for the purpose of affecting a 
decision. (Regulatio_n 18704(c).) In addition, contacting any staff for the purpose of affe.cting a 
decision would be considered using your official position to influence a decision, which is also 
prohibited. (Regulation l 8704(c)( I). Therefore. you may not discuss MAP related matters with 
your assistant, staff, or consultants unless you arc doing so in a ministerial capacity, such as 
carrying out the decisions made by the other members of the Board, without exercising any 
individual judgment. (Regulation 18704(d)(l).) 
In addition. Section 87105 and Regulation 18707 require you to publicly identify each 
type of financial interest involved that gives rise to the disqualifying conflict of interest. The 
identification must be made after the announcement of the agenda item to be discussed or voted 
upon. but before the discussion or vote commences. You must also recuse yourself and leave the 
room after identifying the fonns of conflict. 
EYcl'ptio11s 
A. Personal Interests 
Regulation 18704(d)(2) expressly prm ides an exception where an otherwise disqualified 
official appears in the same manner as any other member of the general public before the 
official's own agency in the course of its prescribed governmental function to represent the 
official's own personal interests.4 
Under Regufation l 8704(d)(2)(A)-(C) "personal interests" include: ''(A) Real ptoperty 
owned emire(r by the official. members of his or her immediate family, or the official and 
members of his or her immediate family: (B) A business entity owned emire(r by the official, 
members of his or her immediate family, or the official and members of his or her immediate 
family: or (C) A business entity over which the official, members of his or her immediate family. 
? Where 1he excep1ion does apply. your comments must be limned to your personal interests. and care 
should be taken to clarify that you are nol acting m any official capacity. Thus. the exception would not pennil you 
10 appear before 1he Board to represent any other person's mteresls or the inlcrests of any group. 


File No. A-15-063 
Page No. 6 
or the official and members of his or her immediate family so/e(v orjoi111(r exercise full direction 
and colltro/." (Emphasis added.) Based on the information provided, none of your interests fit 
within this exception. 
B. Communication with the Public or the Press 
In addition, Regulation 18704(d)(4) provides that where you communicate with the 
general public or the press, such communications are not considered an atte1T1pt to use your 
position to influence a governmental decision, whether you speak regarding your own interest or 
the interests of others. This would include speaking at other public meetings of the city. 
Additionally, the Act would not prohibit you from obtaining information otherwise 
availahle !O the publlc. However, other contacts. including those \Ǚ'ith staff, regarding a decision 
in which you have a conflict of interest may be considered "influencing'' the decision. 
KL:vll 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 3.22-5660. 
By: 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
g'7 
Legal Intern, Legal Division
